title: Summarize a passage in 3 bullets
response:
- Before the 1950s, cargo was shipped via break-bulk methods — sacks, barrels, and crates loaded piece by piece — so that loading a medium-sized ship could take a week and cost around $5.86 per ton.
- In 1956, trucking entrepreneur Malcom McLean sent the converted tanker Ideal X from Newark to Houston carrying 58 uniform metal boxes that moved straight from truck to ship, cutting loading costs to roughly 16 cents per ton.
- The real breakthrough was standardization: after competing container sizes and corner fittings threatened to fragment the industry in the 1960s, international standards agreed by 1968 settled common dimensions and twistlock corner castings, driving shipping costs so low that transport largely stopped mattering to where goods were made and far-flung supply chains became economical.
scoreDetail: fidelity: 9/10, coverage: 9/10, format compliance: 8/10, concision: 6/10 — Facts and coverage are accurate and complete, but the third bullet is a long, clause-stuffed run-on that hurts concision.
